entities.name: WATER HYACINTH
entities.description: An invasive aquatic plant species originating from South America that causes significant concern in Lake Victoria.

entities.name: SOUTH AMERICA
entities.description: The geographical region of origin for the invasive water hyacinth.

entities.name: LAKE VICTORIA
entities.description: A large lake in Kenya where water hyacinth has been introduced and causes ecological concerns.

entities.name: KENYA
entities.description: The country where Lake Victoria is located.

entities.name: WINAM GULF
entities.description: A specific area within Lake Victoria characterized by large annual blooms of water hyacinth.

entities.name: IN SITU METHODS
entities.description: Ground-based monitoring techniques for water hyacinth that are expensive and challenging due to the lake's size.

entities.name: REMOTE SENSING MONITORING METHODS
entities.description: Satellite-based monitoring techniques that offer an alternative to in situ methods for covering vast areas.

entities.name: POLARIMETRIC SYNTHETIC APERTURE RADAR
entities.description: A remote sensing technology (PolSAR) used to map and monitor the spatial and temporal dynamics of water hyacinth.

entities.name: SENTINEL-1 VV-VH DATA
entities.description: Specific polarimetric radar data used within the PolSAR framework to monitor water hyacinth cover.

entities.name: CHANGE DETECTION METHOD
entities.description: A computational approach used to separate clear water from water hyacinth-infested water with high accuracy.

entities.name: OPTIMISATION OF POWER DIFFERENCE
entities.description: A specific technique (OPDiff) used as a basis for the change detection method.

entities.name: MINIMUM EIGENVALUE SELECTION
entities.description: A selection process used in the change detection method to achieve high accuracy in water hyacinth separation.

entities.name: SPATIAL AND TEMPORAL MONITORING
entities.description: The conceptual approach of tracking the distribution and seasonal changes of water hyacinth over time and space.
